title: Illumina DNA Prep - Opentrons v3
author: Opentrons
materials: This protocol automates the [Illumina DNA prep protocol](https://support.illumina.com/content/dam/illumina-support/documents/documentation/chemistry_documentation/illumina_prep/illumina-dna-prep-reference-guide-1000000025416-09.pdf). Illumina DNA prep offers a fast, integrated workflow for a wide range of applications, from human whole-genome sequencing to amplicons, plasmids, and microbial species The protocol allows you to set the number of samples to 8, 16 or 24 (i.e. 8 samples per column, up to 3 columns). Samples are prepared in the wells as shown in the table and figure below, with 30 µL of 100 ng:s of sample DNA in each well. See the [Illumina DNA Prep protocol](https://support.illumina.com/content/dam/illumina-support/documents/documentation/chemistry_documentation/illumina_prep/illumina-dna-prep-reference-guide-1000000025416-09.pdf) for more information about sample input requirements. The user can choose which steps of the protocol they want to run, or skip - see explanation of parameters below. **Plate Moving** The Protocol requires manually transferring the sample plate between the Thermocycler and Magnet 3 times. It starts on the Thermocycler and needs to be moved to the Magnet for the post-Tagmentation washes, and then moved to the Thermocycler for PCR and then back to the Magnet for the post-PCR cleanup. In the script the two positions are handled as sample_plate_mag and sample_plate_thermo; during calibration use an empty plate of the same labware as the sample plate on the magnet position to allow calibration. Explanation of parameters below: * `Number of samples`: 8 (column 1), 16 (column 1, 3), or 24 (column 1, 3, 5) samples (see above). * `Do a dry run?`: Sets the `Use modules?` parameter to `No` (see below). Tips will be returned, incubation steps skipped, and mixes shortened. This parameter is for testing purposes. * `Use modules?`: Runs the protocol without module steps (e.g. thermocycle steps such as incubation and PCR cycles, or the steps us
User seals the plate and the protocol incubates the samples with the mix in the thermocycler, 55 degrees for 15 minutes.
The thermocycler opens, and the user removes the seal.
The protocol performs two ethanol washes.

steps.title: Prepare the thermocycler by setting the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the thermocycler by setting the block temperature to 4 degrees, and the lid to 100 degrees.

steps.title: Add tagmentation mix to the samples
steps.instruction: Add tagmentation mix to the samples

steps.title: Add Tagmentation Stop Buffer to the samples
steps.instruction: Add Tagmentation Stop Buffer to the samples.

steps.title: Seal and incubate the mix at...
steps.instruction: Seal and incubate the mix at 37 degrees for 15 minutes.

steps.title: User removes seal; remove the supernatant...
steps.instruction: User removes seal; remove the supernatant and wash the beads three times with Tagmentation Wash Buffer.

steps.title: Amplification of DNA
steps.instruction: Amplification of DNA: Addition of PCR mix and addition of barcodes.

steps.title: The protocol runs PCR protocol for 5 cycles
steps.instruction: The protocol runs PCR protocol for 5 cycles. This takes 25 minutes to complete. The supernatant is transferred to columns 7, 9 and 11 depending on how many sample columns there are.

steps.title: RSB (resuspension buffer) is added to...
steps.instruction: RSB (resuspension buffer) is added to the bead wells and the supernatant is transferred to the output columns (8, 10, and 12).
